channel.title: Reinvently — Independent AI Research & Analysis
channel.description: Independent analysis of enterprise AI adoption, UK AI policy, model evaluation, and AI engineering tools.

item.title: LLM Benchmark Design: Building the Ed-o-meter
item.description: How I built Featherbench, a single-file LLM eval harness running 28 tasks: one variable held constant, refusals counted as failures, and judge bias measured.

item.title: GLM-5.2 vs Fable 5 vs GPT-5.5: Our 28-Task Eval Results
item.description: GPT-5.5 passed 28 of 28 tasks, GLM-5.2 passed 26, and Claude Fable 5 refused to do 5 of them. Full results, costs, and a refusal deep dive.

item.title: The UK AI Policy Landscape: What Enterprise Leaders Need to Track in 2026
item.description: No UK AI Act exists. Instead: sector regulators, a £500m sovereign compute programme, AI Growth Labs and a Hardware Plan. The 2026 map for enterprise leaders.

item.title: AI Growth Labs: What the UK's Regulatory Sandboxes Mean for Your Sector
item.description: The UK's AI Growth Labs open to legal services this summer, with the ICO and three legal regulators involved. What regulatory sandboxes mean for your sector.

item.title: Is Claude Fable 5 Worth It for Enterprise Coding?
item.description: Claude Fable 5 tops FrontierSWE at 90 percent but costs $10/$50 per MTok, double Opus 4.8. Whether the premium is worth it, and when GLM-5.2 is enough.

item.title: The Agent Governance Gap: $202m Budgets, 26 Percent Cost Visibility
item.description: KPMG's Q2 2026 pulse: enterprises plan $202m average AI spend but only 26 percent see costs in real time. Why the agent governance gap decides who gets ROI.

item.title: How to Sandbox AI Agent Code: Firecracker, OpenSandbox, Docker, SmolVM and nono Compared
item.description: Firecracker, OpenSandbox, Docker Sandboxes, SmolVM and nono compared: four isolation models for AI agent code, from 5 MiB microVMs to zero-setup sandboxes.

item.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration Frameworks Compared: ruflo, Aperant, Sandcastle, Mission Control & Maestro
item.description: ruflo, Aperant, Sandcastle, Mission Control and Maestro compared. Why 58,000 GitHub stars measure attention, not reliability, and which tool fits which job.

item.title: GSD, BMAD, OpenSpec, or GitHub Spec Kit: Choosing the Right AI Development Framework
item.description: GSD, BMAD, OpenSpec and GitHub Spec Kit compared: choosing a spec-driven AI development framework, from the 90,000-star Spec Kit to BMAD's $2,000 monthly cost.

item.title: GitHub Copilot vs Claude Code vs Cursor
item.description: GitHub Copilot, Claude Code and Cursor compared on capability, control and cost, with Claude Code at 46 percent most-loved in a survey of 906 developers.

item.title: GPT-5.5 and the Age of Autonomous Task Completion
item.description: GPT-5.5, tested with nearly 200 partners before its 2026 release, completes complex tasks autonomously. The governance questions to answer before deploying it.

item.title: Claude Memory & Dream: What They Mean for Enterprise AI Adoption
item.description: Claude's Memory and Dream features cut first-pass errors by 97 percent in early deployments. What persistent, self-improving agents mean for enterprise AI.

item.title: UK AI Regulation Tightens: What the ICO Guidance and Parliament's Inquiry Mean for Employers
item.description: Three 2026 developments: an ICO consultation, a Commons inquiry and a DRCF agentic AI paper tighten UK rules on AI at work. What employers should audit now.

item.title: The Enterprise AI Reality Check: Why 79% of Organisations Aren't Seeing ROI
item.description: 79 percent of organisations struggle with AI adoption and only 29 percent see ROI. What WRITER's survey and Stanford's playbook say the leaders do differently.

item.title: UK Sovereign AI: What the Government's £500m Bet Means for Your Organisation
item.description: The UK's £500 million Sovereign AI programme, explained: why five companies controlling 70 percent of global AI compute pushed Britain to act on infrastructure.

item.title: The A2A Protocol: AI Agent Interoperability for Enterprise
item.description: Backed by 150+ organisations, the A2A protocol lets AI agents from different vendors work together. What it means for enterprise architecture and procurement.

item.title: Global AI Adoption: Attitudes and Intensity by Country
item.description: Global AI adoption compared: venture funding passed $100 billion in 2024 while the EU leads on regulation. How attitudes and intensity differ by country.

item.title: Claude Mythos: Anthropic's Most Powerful Model Yet
item.description: Anthropic's Mythos model found a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug. What its security capabilities and Project Glasswing mean for companies planning AI adoption.

item.title: The Top 10 UK Companies Leading on AI
item.description: The 10 UK companies leading on AI in 2026, from DeepMind to Wayve and its $1.05 billion raise, and what their progress signals for the UK technology ecosystem.

item.title: RAG vs GraphRAG: A Practical Comparison
item.description: RAG vs GraphRAG: how Microsoft's open-source knowledge-graph approach, released in 2024, differs from standard retrieval and when each fits your use case.

item.title: Microsoft Copilot Studio vs Azure AI Foundry vs AWS Bedrock
item.description: Copilot Studio, Microsoft Foundry and AWS Bedrock compared: no-code speed, developer depth or 100+ models via one API. Which one fits your organisation.

item.title: Closing the UK's AI Skills Gap
item.description: 73 percent of UK adults have had no AI training at all. Where the shortage bites hardest, what the £400 billion figure really measures, and the funded routes to close it.

item.title: Microsoft Foundry: A Deep Dive
item.description: Microsoft Foundry deep dive: OpenAI and Anthropic models in one governed platform, agents with 1,400+ tool integrations, and when it is the right choice.

item.title: The UK's Government AI Institutes
item.description: A guide to the UK's public AI institutes, from the Alan Turing Institute to the world's first national AI safety body: what each does and why it matters.

item.title: Generative AI Adoption by Industry: Use Cases, Data and What Comes Next
item.description: Generative AI adoption by industry: 71 percent of organisations now use it regularly, returning $3.70 per $1 invested. The use cases and data, sector by sector.

item.title: The Rise of AI in Web Development
item.description: 84 percent of developers now use or plan to use AI tools. Where the gains in web development are real, where they are overstated, and what to do about it.
